commonContract.universe: Point-in-time union of SPY and QQQ constituents
commonContract.exit: Sell 50% at six months; extend the remainder only when weekly close >= MA10 and RSI14 >= 50; then exit on two weekly closes below MA10 or at 12 months

classificationAudit.finding: The frozen 57-label field mixes broad GICS sectors and granular industry labels, so it is reproducible but not one coherent taxonomy.

coherentMorningstar.classification: Morningstar sector for sector scoring and Morningstar industry group for Leader2 grouping, applied to every constituent

productionCompatibleBase.classification: Frozen legacy label for mapped current names and point-in-time Morningstar fallback for historical-only names

decision.recommendedOperationalState: Keep Score A active and Score C candidate; pause the planned automatic August promotion until taxonomy and capital-path fixes are retested.
reasons: Under a coherent Morningstar taxonomy, C beat A by only 0.04 percentage points while its MDD was 14.92 points worse.
Under the production-compatible long baseline, C beat A by only 4.64 percentage points and its MDD was 1.54 points worse.
At 25 bps cost per side, C trailed A by 44.02 percentage points and skipped 85 of 376 intended buys.
With one extra execution day and a five-trading-day membership lag, C kept only an 8.06-point return edge and still had a deeper MDD.
Removing the two best lots did not erase C's small edge, so the main weakness is taxonomy and cash-path sensitivity rather than one isolated winner.

requiredBeforeReconsideration: Replace the mixed frozen 57-label field with one versioned, coherent sector and industry taxonomy.
Reserve cash for future monthly slots and estimated fees so fee changes do not alter which signals can be executed.
Re-run the 2010-2026 PIT test with the repaired taxonomy and capital allocator before any parameter tuning.
After the repaired backtest passes, collect at least 6-12 months of A/C shadow fills before calling C fully validated.
limitations: QuantConnect prices available in this run ended on 2026-04-13.
ETF constituent history is monthly before January 2015 and can be delayed by up to one week.
QQQ is a continuously invested benchmark while the strategy ramps cash and can skip buys, so the comparison is a practical opportunity-cost benchmark rather than a risk-matched portfolio.
No 6-12 month untouched forward shadow period exists yet.
This research result does not itself change the public API, Android execution policy, or current A/C status.
